supervisor.rationale: Phase is `review` with `pre_pr_review` unsatisfied, which is precisely the requirement `/deep-review` auto-satisfies via its PostToolUse skill mapping. Running deep-review first is the canonical next step before the narrower `/codex-review` pass.

findings.title: `*` dirty marker committed into git_hash YAML field
findings.body: Diff lands `git_hash: 3a3f257*` (trailing `*`) into the .md.j2 source. The `*` denotes transient 'locally modified' display state, not a value that should be committed. CHANGELOG acknowledges this as a known limitation, but the workaround (commit then re-run) was not applied; every other git_hash in this diff is a clean 7-hex value.
findings.suggested_fix: Commit pending edits, then re-run `./update-plugin-versions.sh` so git_hash becomes a clean hex value. Or strip the trailing `*` manually as a touch-up.

findings.title: Pre-commit staging hint widened from `-u` may over-stage untracked files
findings.body: Changing the hint from `git add -u plugins/requirements-framework/` to `git add plugins/requirements-framework/` correctly catches newly-rendered .md siblings of new .md.j2 templates, but also stages every other untracked file under the tree, which can silently sneak into a commit.
findings.suggested_fix: Use explicit globs (`git add -u ... && git add ...*.md.j2 ...*.md`) or add a one-line warning recommending `git status` review first.

report.summary: Diff is dominated by version metadata refreshes and byte-identical .md/.md.j2 template pairs; the MD5 gate plus StrictUndefined plus keep_trailing_newline=True give high confidence the conversion preserved runtime behavior. Substantive bits are correct: the SKILL.md case-sensitivity fix repairs a silent zero-match bug, the new regression test promotes a manual check to CI, and the CHANGELOG corrects an earlier-wrong claim about keep_trailing_newline. Two minor cosmetics flagged.

aggregator.skipped_reason: single worker; aggregator is moot for N=1 reports

langfuse.note: Open Langfuse UI to see auto-instrumented spans (supervisor + worker, each a top-level trace).
